items.question: The safest place to fit a feature selector in a supervised workflow is:
options: On the full dataset before any split
Inside a Pipeline, refit on each training fold only
On the test set to maximize relevant features
After evaluating on the test set
items.answer: Inside a Pipeline, refit on each training fold only
items.explanation: Selectors must learn from training data only; Pipeline + CV refits the selector per fold and prevents label leakage from validation or test rows.

items.question: Which artifact best preserves train/serve parity for selected columns?
options: A notebook cell listing column names
A serialized fitted Pipeline including the selector step
items.answer: A serialized fitted Pipeline including the selector step
items.explanation: The fitted pipeline stores the support mask and applies it consistently at inference.

items.question: RFECV is primarily used to:
options: Choose how many features to keep using cross-validation
Impute missing values in wide tables
items.answer: Choose how many features to keep using cross-validation
items.explanation: RFECV wraps RFE with CV to pick subset size without tuning k on a held-out test set.

items.question: For deeper coverage of mutual information scores and L1 selection math, you should read:
items.explanation: The dimensionality-reduction companion topic owns filter/wrapper/embedded statistical detail; this topic focuses on pipeline integration.
